content.full_text:
January 31, 1992

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Chief, Technical Assistance & Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Request for Advice

Dear Jeanne:

Pursuant to our recent telephone conversation, this letter will request informal assistance from the Commission staff on the following issue.

Our firm represents a client which hires a university professor to testify on its behalf before a legislative committee. The professor, who lives in the East, is compensated at a level which requires him to register and report as a lobbying firm/lobbyist. His activity in this regard consists solely of coming to California on the day of the legislative hearing, signing and filing his lobby registration forms, providing public testimony before the legislative committee, signing and filing a lobbying firm/lobbyist termination and returning back East that day. He files a quarterly lobbying firm/lobbyist disclosure form showing the payment from his client, no activity expenses and no campaign contributions. The professor does not meet with any legislators or legislative staff other than providing the public testimony.

Since the professor's activity is of such limited scope (and if he were giving such testimony before a State agency, he would not even qualify as a lobbyist), we ask whether he is required to attend the orientation course conducted by the Legislative Ethics Committees on relevant ethical issues and laws relating to lobbying, as proscribed by Government Code §86103(d)(2)?

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard  
January 31, 1992  
Page 2

If you need any additional information regarding this matter do not hesitate to contact me.

Kind regards,

Kirk Alan Pessner  
Paralegal Administrator  
Political Reports Unit

KAP/dc
